summary: Small assembly-only compile fix for Sandbag-related function pointer references in `asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s`. A table near `lbl_803D3998` was updated to reference `func_8014FC20`, `func_8014FC24`, `func_8014FC28`, and `func_8014FC48` instead of the corresponding `lbl_` symbols, resolving compilation issues caused by using label names where function symbols were expected.
what_changed: In `asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s`, four `.4byte` entries in a data table after `lbl_803D3998` were changed from `lbl_8014FC20`, `lbl_8014FC24`, `lbl_8014FC28`, and `lbl_8014FC48` to `func_8014FC20`, `func_8014FC24`, `func_8014FC28`, and `func_8014FC48`.
The surrounding table still includes constants such as `0x00000127`, `NULL`, `0x01400000`, and a later pointer to `func_800761C8`; only the four Sandbag-related function pointer symbol names changed.
The PR had no body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the provided slice, so the rationale is inferred from the title and diff: fixing compilation from Sandbag functions.
smart_moves: Used the existing `func_` symbol convention for callable assembly routines instead of leaving table entries pointed at raw `lbl_` labels.
Made a minimal targeted assembly change: four symbol references changed, no unrelated table layout or data values altered.
Preserved addresses in the symbol names while correcting the symbol class/prefix, reducing risk of changing behavior while restoring buildability.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly data table stores function pointers, use the project’s exported function symbol names (`func_...`) rather than local/generic labels (`lbl_...`) if the symbols are intended to be resolved as functions during assembly/linking.
Compile failures can come from stale symbol names in handwritten or generated assembly tables after functions are named or promoted from labels to function symbols.
For decompilation cleanup, verify that pointer tables referencing code addresses are updated consistently when labels are renamed to functions.
naming_conventions: `func_XXXXXXXX` denotes a function symbol at address `0xXXXXXXXX`.
`lbl_XXXXXXXX` denotes a generic label at address `0xXXXXXXXX`; it may be inappropriate for function pointer table entries once the target has a function symbol.
This PR demonstrates replacing `lbl_8014FC20`-style names with `func_8014FC20`-style names for Sandbag-related callable entries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For assembly compile fixes, keep the data-table byte layout unchanged and only correct symbol references when the underlying addresses are unchanged.
In `.4byte` function pointer tables, symbol prefix correctness matters for assembly/link resolution even when the numeric address suffix is identical.

A safe tactic is to replace label references with known function symbols one-for-one: `lbl_8014FC20` -> `func_8014FC20`, `lbl_8014FC24` -> `func_8014FC24`, `lbl_8014FC28` -> `func_8014FC28`, `lbl_8014FC48` -> `func_8014FC48`.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile or rename Sandbag functions around `8014FC20`, `8014FC24`, `8014FC28`, and `8014FC48`.
Search for other `.4byte lbl_` entries in function pointer tables that should use `func_` symbols to avoid similar compile issues.
Check whether `asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s` was later split or converted to C and whether this table received a typed struct definition.
